criteria.criterion: A National Society has been selected from the dropdown
The Application is done in the right type of DREF funding request template (Assessment, Response, or Anticipation)
The type of disaster is clearly specified (Flood, Earthquake, Cyclone, Drought, Epidemic, etc.)
Onset type is appropriately selected (Sudden Onset or Slow Onset)
The event is clearly classified according to the IFRC Crisis Categorisation (Yellow, Orange, or Red)
The affected country(ies) have been clearly specified
The targeted areas are clearly defined with affected region(s) or province(s) specified
DREF title clearly explains the disaster/crisis type and area of focus
A clear decision has been made regarding whether an emergency appeal is planned (Yes or No)
A map of the targeted areas is available in the DREF application showing geographic scope
Supporting documentation/cover image has been uploaded demonstrating the situation
The date of disaster/trigger date is specified in the application
The scope and scale of the event is clearly detailed and provides enough information aligned to the assigned crisis categorization
People in need (PIN) figures are provided or marked as optional with clear definition
Estimated male affected population is specified with numbers
Estimated female affected population is specified with numbers
Estimated girls under 18 are included in demographic breakdown
Estimated boys under 18 are included in demographic breakdown
The event is clearly explained in terms of when, where and what occurred (or is expected to occur in anticipatory actions)
It is clear if information is from needs assessment by NS or secondary data with clear description of sources (e.g. UN, NGOs, authorities)
Photos have been included to show the damages caused by the disaster and/or National Society actions
It is clear whether the National Society has started responding, and the date when they started their activities
Actions done by Movement and external partners, including support to the National Society, are explained in the application
Coordination with ICRC/Participating National Societies and other organisations is well explained with their specific contributions
ICRC presence and direct support for the emergency response are documented
It is clear whether government has requested international assistance
Actions taken by national authorities are clearly explained
Actions by UN and other external partners are clearly documented
General coordination mechanisms in place, if any, are mentioned and explained
Needs analysis is based on actual assessment by National Society or secondary data sources are clearly identified
The needs analysis is focused on the needs of the affected population and are clearly detailed by sector
Any current gaps of information regarding the needs of the population are mentioned with explanation of how they will be covered
Operation objective clearly defines the targeted population, targeted areas, type of support/services, and planned length of the operation
The rationale clearly explains the connection between the identified needs and the proposed operation
Number of persons to be targeted and areas to be reached are clearly defined and coherent with identified needs
Selection criteria are clearly explained ensuring that the support reaches those most in need and most vulnerable
All proposed activities are clearly linked to specific consequences of the disaster/crisis and are aligned with Sphere standards
Capacity of the National Society to implement the proposed activities is clearly explained
If the NS is already implementing other DREF operations, explanations on how current capacity is organised are provided
For any key humanitarian needs not covered by this DREF, it is explained how they will be covered by others or acknowledged as a gap
For each identified risk, mitigation measures are clearly explained to reduce the impact on the operation
National Society has anti-fraud and corruption policy in place
National Society has prevention of sexual exploitation and abuse policy
National Society has child protection/safeguarding policy
National Society has whistleblower protection policy
National Society has anti-sexual harassment policy
Security and safety concerns for the operation and National Society staff/volunteers are identified with mitigation measures in place
All sector budgets are coherent with proposed activities, number of persons targeted, and operational indicators
Child safeguarding risk analysis assessment has been completed
Budget summary document has been uploaded
Requested funding amount in CHF is specified
Human resources (volunteers, permanent staff, response units from Partner National Societies) required for implementation are clearly explained
Volunteer team diversity (gender, age, cultural) is described
If Surge personnel are requested, the needed roles and positions are clearly specified and justified
Procurement responsibility and approach are clearly explained
Monitoring mechanisms and systems are documented
National Society communication strategy for the operation is described
Date of National Society application is recorded
Date of submission to Geneva (GVA) is recorded
Date of approval is recorded
Operation timeframe in months is clearly specified
End date of operation is calculated and recorded
Date of publishing is recorded
Appeal code is documented
GLIDE number is documented
IFRC Appeal Manager contact information (name, title, email, phone) is complete
IFRC Project Manager contact information (name, title, email, phone) is complete
National Society contact information (name, title, email, phone) is complete
IFRC Focal Point for Emergency contact information is complete
DREF Regional Focal Point contact information is complete
Media contact information is complete
National Societies' Integrity Focal Point contact information is complete
National Society hotline number is provided
